titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Clayton Eshleman Papers
titlestmt.author: by Jennifer Meehan, Annalise Hennessey

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 7666395.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday August 5, 2023 at 03:02

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection consists of material created and accumulated by Clayton Eshleman in the course of his activities as a poet, author, translator, and editor, and primarily documents Eshleman's literary career from circa 1992 to 2008 and the publication of Sulfur magazine from circa 1990 to 2000. Material includes notes, drafts, and manuscripts for Eshleman's works of poetry and prose (published after 1994) and for works translated by Eshleman; proofs for issues 28-45/46 of Sulfur , as well as correspondence, unpublished manuscripts, and other production material; correspondence with family, authors, poets, and colleagues; notebooks; computer media; and other papers.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Ken Lopez Bookseller on various funds, 2005, 2009, 2015, 2018, and 2019. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open for research.
Boxes 50, 85, 87 (born digital): Restricted fragile materials. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 86 (audiovisual materials): Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Clayton Eshleman Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Clayton Eshleman Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, and competing priorities. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization, upon acquisition. Further organization and description was carried out in 2011. Various acquisitions associated with the collection have not been merged and organized as a whole. Each acquisition is described separately in the contents list below and titled according to month and year of acquisition.

Descriptive information found in the Collection Contents section is drawn in large part from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This collection includes material formerly classed as Uncat MSS 836 and Uncat MSS 1193.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

bioghist.head: Clayton Eshleman
bioghist.p: Clayton Eshleman was born in Indianapolis, Indiana, in 1935. He earned a B.A. in philosophy and an M.A. in creative writing, both from Indiana University. He is the author of numerous collections of poetry and prose, including Under World Arrest (1994), Companion Spider (2002), An Alchemist with One Eye on the Fire (2006), and Reciprocal Distillations (2007), and has translated the work of César Vallejo and Aimé Césaire, among others. He founded and edited the literary magazines Caterpillar (1967-1973) and Sulfur (1981-2000).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of material created and accumulated by Clayton Eshleman in the course of his activities as a poet, author, translator, and editor, and primarily documents Eshleman's literary career from circa 1992 to 2008 and the publication of Sulfur magazine from circa 1990 to 2000. Material includes notes, drafts, and manuscripts for Eshleman's works of poetry and prose (published after 1994) and for works translated by Eshleman; proofs for issues 28-45/46 of Sulfur , as well as correspondence, unpublished manuscripts, and other production material; correspondence with family, authors, poets, and colleagues, including Eshleman's wife, Caryl Eshleman, and Herbert Lust; notebooks; computer media; and other papers.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five groupings: December 2005 Acquisition, June 2009 Acquisition, July 2015 Acquisition, December 2018 Acquisition, and December 2019 Acquisition.
Material within this collection has been organized by acquisition reflecting the fact that the collection has been acquired in increments over time.

Researchers should note that material within each acquisition overlaps with and/or relates to material found in other acquisitions. For instance, correspondence and writings can be found in both groupings. In order to locate all relevant material within this collection, researchers will need to consult each acquisition described in the Collection Contents section.
Researchers should also note that similar material can be arranged differently in each acquisition, depending on how the material was organized when it was received by the library.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five series: Sulfur Production Files; Poetry and Prose; Translations; Correspondence and Subject Files; and Other Papers.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Original computer disks are restricted. Copies of electronic files are available through Access Services.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title.

did.unittitle: "Materials from Erratics - set aside Sept 15 '99" and other material

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by author or title.

did.unittitle: Notebook of a Return to Native Land by Aimé Césaire
Notebook of a Return to Native Land by Aimé Césaire
Rich 2000 - materials relating to Adrienne Rich's 2000 EMU visit
Correspondence Getneon CE and Adrienne Rich
Cave articles and proposals, destinations article material

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 50 (computer disk): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: 1945 from America, A History in Verse Vol. 2 1940-1969 Quark Xpress 3.31 figural book font

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four series: Poetry and Prose; Translations; Correspondence; and Notebooks.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title of work.

title.part: An Alchemist with One Eye on the Fire

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title of work.

title.part: The Complete Poetry of César Vallejo
The Complete Poetry of César Vallejo

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Printed email correspondence is arranged alphabetically by surname of correspondent. General correspondence and postcards are arranged in files at the end of the series.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Ken Lopez Bookseller on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2015.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This addition consists of writings, correspondence, printed material, teaching materials, artwork, and other papers by or relating to Clayton Eshleman.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials relating to the book Anticline, including correspondence, notes, and corrected typescripts.

did.unittitle: Notebook of translations of Antonin Artaud, Bernard Bador, Michel Deguy, Robert Marteau, and Guy Etienne

scopecontent.p: Unpublished writings.

scopecontent.p: Introduction and photocopied poems.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writings, notes, and correspondence relating to Eshleman and Klein's translation of Bei Dao.

Writings, include rejected submissions, notes, and related correspondence and clippings

Price of Experience and An Anatomy of the Night

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drafts, corrections, and an interview with Jared Demick.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writings consisting of copies with corrections and highlights and cutouts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, receipts and related materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contracts with related materials. Also includes reviews from publications dating back to the 1960s.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writings, drafts, proofs, correspondence, and related materials.

did.unittitle: Translation of Aime Cesaire 1939 notebook, Return to the Native Land, proof with notes

scopecontent.p: Poems not used or revised before publication.

scopecontent.p: Poems not used or revised before publication.

scopecontent.p: Poem inscribed to Herbert and Virginia from Eshleman.

scopecontent.p: Records of daily activities, appointments, and travel.

scopecontent.p: Records of daily activities, appointments, and travel.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drafts, worksheets, and research materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Teaching material and related correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence and related materials.

scopecontent.p: Legal materials and related correspondence.

scopecontent.p: Printed email correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed email correspondence and other correspondence with related writings and other materials.

did.unittitle: Published and unpublished interviews, essays, notes, and reviews.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes notes on Tejada's Mirrors for Gold, Israeli invasion of the Gaza Strip, for a class What is a Poet?, Leakey's The Origin of Humankind, review of Neruda's The Grapes and the Wind, The Rhinecliff Station, The Color of Time, notes on Avant-Garde for a 2002 panel, notes on Rhisomic Identities", Stages of Regeneration, Talk on Surrealism, Proteus and Poetic Experiment, notes for a talk on contemporary American poetry, and other materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence, business papers, translations, and other materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published and unpublished writings and related correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Translations

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes, writings, and copies.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Research notes and writings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebook and annotated copy of Mazzotti's work.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes and photocopies.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Worksheets with notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Draft and notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Presentation and talk materials with notes and related correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writings, notes, correspondence, interviews, and other materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebooks include writings, notes, reminders, and sketches.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Research materials, notes, and writings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writings, travel information, printed ephemera, and related materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writings, travel information, printed ephemera, and related materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writings, travel information, printed ephemera, and other related materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Classroom teaching materials from Eastern Michigan University and University of California Los Angeles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes notes and photographs. Also includes an undated drawing depicting jazz titled "Bop."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Worksheets and notes for poems 1 to 4.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes writings, notes, and worksheets for rejected poems, Explanation and Lautreamont Disneyland, Position Paper, Eternity at Domme, Kerlescan, Anticline and An Anatomy of Night, and Tree Grows out of Hell.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writings and notes from an article about Padgett published in "Companion Spider."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed material from Eastern Michigan University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes, schedules, addresses, and related materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Articles by archeologists Margaret Conkey, Paul Bahn, Barbara MacLeod, and paleolithic art expert Jean Clottes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Titles include Journey Through the Ice Age, M The Man Who Became Caravaggio, The Creative Explosion, Ten Years Under the Earth, Myth From The Ice Age to Mickey Mouse, The Journal the of Charles Olson Archives, Modernism and Negritude, The Time Falling Bodies Take to Light, October #37, and The Mind in the Cave.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Titles include Les Cavernes Du Volp, The Cave of Lascaux: The Final Photographs, and Lascaux or the Birth of Art.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Titles include Beyond Art, Secrets of the Ice Age, and The Cave Artists.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Titles include Pan and the Nightmare, The Road to Eleusis, The Cosmic Serpent, Blood Rites, Blood Bread and Roses: How Menstruation Created the World, Francis Bacon: Anatomy of An Enigma, The Complete Writings of William Blake, Night Life Night Language, and Sleep and Dreams.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Titles include The Gate of Horn, This Tree Grows Out of Hell, Leaves of Grass, The Murder of Christ, Fearful Symmetry, Love's Body, The Wise Wound: Menstruation and Everywoman, The Mass Psychology of Fascism, Inter Views: Conversations with Laura Pozzo, Re-Visioning Psychology, The Dream and the Underworld, The Elegy on Hats, Mirrors for Gold, American Poetry Since 1950: Innovators and Outsiders, Shamanism, The Woman's Encyclopedia of Myths and Secrets, The Collected Poems of Wallace Stevens, The Collected Books of Jack Spicer, and The Discovery of The Orgone: The Function of the Orgasm.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Titles include Leon Golub: Existential Activist and Painter, Brittany, The Maximus Poems, Twins and the Double, and The Maximus Poems: Volume Three, Maximus Poems: I.V., V., VI.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Titles include Healing Fiction, two copies of Ghost Dance: The Origins of Religion, Caravaggion, and The Force of Character and the Lasting Life.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Material removed from cataloged printed items
Entering the Fiftieth Millennium , text of a talk given by Gary Snyder for the 1996 Modern Languages Association Conference Presidents' Panel; photocopy with corrections in Eshleman's hand

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This portion of the collection includes audiovisual materials and computer media featuring poetry readings by Clayton Eshleman.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged in two subseries: Audiocassettes and Computer Media.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by file title.

accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Reading at the Kootenay School of Writing
(You and Robert K) / (Robert K and Paul)

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile materials. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This portion of the collection includes writings, correspondence, photographs, ephemera, and other papers by or relating to Clayton Eshleman. Writings include ephemera, drafts, translations, notes, proofs, lectures, essays, and various unpublished materials.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into eight subseries: Writings; Translations; Caterpillar and Sulfur ; Correspondence; Interviews, Lectures, and Events; Other Papers; Photographs; and Annotated Books.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries includes writings by or about Eshleman, including book reviews, articles, book introductions, and various notebooks.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by file title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries includes materials relating to the Sulfur Anthology and one copy of the Caterpillar Anthology .

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by file title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 4 folders

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of the correspondence of Eshleman with his friends and colleagues, including Horst Haack, Herbert Lust, and Antonet O'Toole, and correspondence with immediate and extended family, including Eshleman's wife, Caryl Eshleman.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by correspondent or file title, with unidentified correspondence filed at the end of the sequence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries includes materials relating to readings, talks, lectures, interviews, and events by or with Eshleman.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: Cave art lectures and essays, etc.

scopecontent.p: 2 folders

scopecontent.p: 2 folders

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries includes royalties, contracts, non-printed materials, and other personal papers.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by folder title.

scopecontent.p: 5 folders

scopecontent.p: 3 folders

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries features photographs of Eshleman, family and friends, colleagues, and artwork.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials arranged in original order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains materials Eshleman used to conduct research for his works.

arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by file title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of various books annotated by Eshleman.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by author or file title.

did.unittitle: Ashe, Geoffrey, Camelot and the Vision of Albion
Duerr, Hans Peter, Dreamtime: Concerning the Boundary Between Wilderness and Civilization ; translated by Felicitas D. Goodman.
Eliade, Mircea, Myths, Dreams, and Mysteries: the Encounter Between Contemporary Faiths and Archaic Realities ; translated by Philip Mairet.
Webster, Brenda S., Blake's Prophetic Psychology
